narrative: The Riverside specialty-chemicals plant is a Large Quantity Generator (EPA ID TXR000123456) generating roughly 4,200 kg/month of F003/F005 spent solvents. Drums are dated upon first drop into the satellite accumulation area, capped at 55 gallons per stream, and moved to the central 90-day accumulation pad within 3 days of reaching capacity. Each container bears the 'Hazardous Waste' marking, accumulation start date, and DOT proper shipping name; weekly inspections are logged on the EPA-style checklist with zero gaps in the past 24 weeks. Outbound shipments use the e-Manifest system, with the LDR notification and waste-code-specific treatment standard attached to every manifest; signed copies are retained for 3+ years. The on-site 5,000-gal tank sits within a coated concrete secondary containment sized to 110% of capacity. The Part B permit is current through 2029, the contingency plan is on file with the local LEPC, and all shipping signatories hold current 49 CFR 172.704 hazmat training certificates.
